itinerary.title: Hanoi Discovery – Temples, Legends & Colonial Charm
itinerary.description: Your full-day exploration begins at the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, the resting place of the revered leader. Nearby, his House on Stilts offers a modest glimpse into the lifestyle of a man who guided a nation.At the One Pillar Pagoda, dating back to 1049, you’ll see one of Vietnam’s most iconic religious sites—symbolizing purity rising from a lotus pond. The peaceful Tran Quoc Pagoda, Hanoi’s oldest Buddhist temple, stands gracefully by West Lake.After lunch, stroll through the poetic grounds of the

itinerary.title: Ninh Binh Wonders – Limestone Landscapes & Ancient Capitals
itinerary.description: Leave behind the lively rhythm of modern Hanoi and venture south into the serene, poetic landscapes of Ninh Binh—a region often hailed as “Halong Bay on land.” Your journey begins in Hoa Lu, the ancient capital of Vietnam, where timeworn temples rest beneath towering karst cliffs, and the air seems to carry whispers of bygone kings and noble warriors. Wander through the sacred grounds of the Dinh and Le Dynasty Temples, gracefully nestled in a lush valley embraced by nature’s dramatic grandeur.C
activities: Wander through the sacred grounds of the Dinh and Le Dynasty Temples, gracefully nestled in a lush valley embraced by nature’s dramatic grandeur

itinerary.title: Hanoi – Halong Bay
itinerary.description: Leave the bustle of Hanoi behind as you journey through the Red River Delta, passing by vibrant rice fields, riverside villages, and farmers guiding their water buffalo. Arrive at Tuan Chau Harbor, where your elegant cruise vessel awaits to carry you through the magical seascape of Halong Bay and the tranquil waters of Lan Ha Bay.As your boat glides past towering limestone pillars rising from emerald waters, enjoy a freshly prepared seafood lunch served on deck. In the afternoon, paddle a kayak
activities: As your boat glides past towering limestone pillars rising from emerald waters, enjoy a freshly prepared seafood lunch served on deck
In the afternoon, paddle a kayak

itinerary.title: Halong Bay - Hanoi
itinerary.description: Begin your morning with a gentle Tai Chi session on the cruise deck, as the first light of day bathes the bay’s majestic limestone peaks in gold. After a hearty breakfast, set out to explore one of the area’s highlights—perhaps the impressive Cave, known for its striking stalactites and dramatic stone formations, or visit a peaceful fishing village, where the rhythms of coastal life unfold amidst scenic surroundings. Each experience offers a unique window into the natural and cultural beauty of

itinerary.title: Hanoi - Fly to Dong Hoi – Phong Nha – Paradise Caves – Dong Hoi
itinerary.description: After breakfast and check out hotel, our driver come to pick you up and transfer to airport for your flight to Dong Hoi. When you arrive in Dong Hoi, we'll pick you up and start your trip.Phong Nha Cave and Paradise Cave are the two most renowned caves in Phong Nha Ke Bang National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site due to its geological significance.Both caves possess an enchanting allure in the "Kingdom of the Caves," Phong Nha Ke Bang National Park. itinerary.title: Dong Hoi – DMZ Battlefield – Vinh Moc Tunnels – Hue
itinerary.description: Depart from Dong Hoi for Quang Tri Province, where you'll visit Vinh Moc Tunnels, a significant site from the Vietnamese Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) during the Vietnam War. Explore the historic Hien Luong Bridge, which spans the Ben Hai River, serving as the border between North and South Vietnam. Afterward, you will be transferred to Hue.Highlights:Visit the historic Vinh Moc Tunnels, a remarkable underground village from the Vietnam War.Meals, Activities & StayHue Breakfast at the dining lounge
activities: Explore the historic Hien Luong Bridge, which spans the Ben Hai River, serving as the border between North and South Vietnam

itinerary.title: Imperial Echoes – Citadels, Pagodas & Royal Tombs
itinerary.description: Begin your journey through Vietnam’s royal past at the grand Imperial Citadel, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Stroll through its moss-lined gates, ancient corridors, and lotus ponds that once echoed with imperial pageantry. These grounds were once home to emperors of the Nguyen Dynasty, whose legacies still linger in the air.Continue to the iconic Thien Mu Pagoda, a historic Buddhist sanctuary perched on a bluff above the Perfume River. Its seven-tiered Phuoc Duyen Tower is a symbol of Hue, and t

itinerary.title: Coastal Route – Hai Van Pass to Historic Hoi An
itinerary.description: Depart Hue and journey south along the picturesque Hai Van Pass, often called the “Ocean Cloud Pass.” This winding ribbon of road climbs between forested mountains and the East Sea, offering spectacular panoramas at every turn. Pause at the summit to take in the sweeping coastal view, a favorite spot for photographers and travel lovers alike.Continue through Da Nang, Vietnam’s third-largest city. In the afternoon, arrive in the ancient town of Hoi An, a UNESCO-listed treasure where lanterns sway

itinerary.title: Ancient Rhythms – Village Life & Lantern Streets of Hoi An
itinerary.description: Begin your morning in the historic heart of Hoi An, a UNESCO World Heritage Site that once thrived as a bustling Southeast Asian trading port. Walk through the vibrant morning market, where colors, aromas, and voices fill the air.Next, journey to Cam Thanh Village and step into a round bamboo coracle boat. Float gently through a peaceful water coconut forest, where fishermen cast nets and life moves with the tide. Then, head to Tra Que Herb Village, where you’ll don the attire of a Vietnamese fa

itinerary.title: Free Time in Hoi An
itinerary.description: Enjoy a leisurely breakfast at the hotel and spend your day at your own pace. Hoi An, often referred to as the "Pearl Island," offers stunning beaches, beautiful islands, and deserted shores. itinerary.title: Hoi An – Da Nang airport - Fly to Ho Chi Minh – A Fusion of Heritage & Hustle
itinerary.description: After breakfast, we'll pick you up and transfer you to the airport for your flight to Ho Chi Minh City. Upon arrival at Tan Son Nhat International Airport, be warmly greeted by your local guide holding a personalized signboard. As you transfer to your hotel in the vibrant heart of the city, catch your first impressions of Ho Chi Minh City where sleek skyscrapers rise beside French-era villas, and the hum of motorbikes weaves through a city that never sleeps. Wide boulevards, tree-shaded parks, a

itinerary.title: Life on Water – Mekong Delta Exploration
itinerary.description: Leave Saigon behind for a full-day journey into the heart of the Mekong Delta, Vietnam’s “rice bowl.” Travel south to Ben Tre, a peaceful province known for its coconut palms and maze of canals. Board a wooden motorboat and cruise along the Ham Luong River, passing stilt houses and cargo boats. Stop at family-run workshops to see how locals produce coconut candy, bricks, and handwoven mats using traditional methods.Continue your adventure with a xe lôi (motor cart) ride through tranquil country
activities: Leave Saigon behind for a full-day journey into the heart of the Mekong Delta, Vietnam’s “rice bowl
” Travel south to Ben Tre, a peaceful province known for its coconut palms and maze of canals

Stop at family-run workshops to see how locals produce coconut candy, bricks, and handwoven mats using traditional methods
Continue your adventure with a xe lôi (motor cart) ride through tranquil country

itinerary.title: Floating Markets & Return to Vibrant Saigon
itinerary.description: Rise early for a boat ride to the bustling Cai Rang Floating Market, a colorful spectacle of trade where vendors sell produce directly from their boats. Watch as pineapples, pumpkins, and coconuts change hands, and sample a steaming bowl of hu tieu (local noodle soup) right from a floating kitchen.After exploring the market, visit a rice noodle factory to observe how this staple food is handcrafted. On the return journey to Ho Chi Minh City, stop at Binh Thuy Ancient House, a beautifully preserv

itinerary.title: Farewell Vietnam – Memories to Carry Home
itinerary.description: Savor your final moments in Vietnam. Enjoy a quiet morning with a stroll through Tao Dan Park, or explore a local coffee shop for one last ca phe sua da (Vietnamese iced coffee). Depending on your flight time, you may stop at a market for souvenirs or revisit a favorite café. At the appointed time, your driver will transfer you to Tan Son Nhat International Airport for your onward journey.Highlight: Farewell to a land of grace, memory, and wonder.Meals, Activities & StayHo Chi Minh Breakfast at
